items.title: Israel orders more than 500,000 people to evacuate Beirut’s southern suburbs
items.standfirst: Instruction comes as Israel continues to bomb Lebanon and Iran, while Tehran launches retaliatory strikesMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe Israeli military has ordered the entire population of Beirut’s southern suburbs to evacuate, as it continued to bomb Lebanon and Iran, while Tehran launched retaliatory strikes against Israel and US bases across the region.An Israel Defense Forces spokesperson told all residents of Beirut’s southern suburbs – more than 500,000 people – to “save your lives and evacuate your homes immediately”, before Israel launched airstrikes on what he described as Hezbollah targets. The area covered by the order included several hospitals and government ministries. Continue reading...
items.content: Instruction comes as Israel continues to bomb Lebanon and Iran, while Tehran launches retaliatory strikesMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe Israeli military has ordered the entire population of Beirut’s southern suburbs to evacuate, as it continued to bomb Lebanon and Iran, while Tehran launched retaliatory strikes against Israel and US bases across the region.An Israel Defense Forces spokesperson told all residents of Beirut’s southern suburbs – more than 500,000 people – to “save your lives and evacuate your homes immediately”, before Israel launched airstrikes on what he described as Hezbollah targets. The area covered by the order included several hospitals and government ministries. Continue reading...

items.title: Israel orders people to leave swathe of Beirut
items.standfirst: Israel warned residents to leave Beirut's southern suburbs, including Hezbollah-controlled areas, on Thursday, prompting an exodus from a swathe of the capital, which a far-right Israeli minister said would soon resemble parts of Gaza. Suggesting a major escalation looms in Israel's ​offensive against the Iran-backed ‌Hezbollah, an Israeli military spokesperson told residents of the southern suburbs to move east and north, posting a map ⁠showing four large districts of the capital he said they must leave.
items.content: Israel warned residents to leave Beirut's southern suburbs, including Hezbollah-controlled areas, on Thursday, prompting an exodus from a swathe of the capital, which a far-right Israeli minister said would soon resemble parts of Gaza. Suggesting a major escalation looms in Israel's ​offensive against the Iran-backed ‌Hezbollah, an Israeli military spokesperson told residents of the southern suburbs to move east and north, posting a map ⁠showing four large districts of the capital he said they must leave.

ai_summary: Israel orders evacuation of Beirut southern suburbs
Over 500000 people affected by the order
Israeli military targets Hezbollah-controlled areas

Residents warned to save lives and evacuate homes

clusters.title: War in the Middle East: Global reactions and military deployments
clusters.neutral_headline: War In Middle East Widen With Global Reactions

items.title: War in the Middle East: Global reactions and military deployments
items.standfirst: Six days after the United States and Israel launched strikes on Iran, the conflict is widening. Israel has carried out strikes in Lebanon, while Washington is asking Iraq’s Kurds for support. FRANCE 24's Catherine Viette takes a look at the reactions and military deployments since Saturday.
items.content: Six days after the United States and Israel launched strikes on Iran, the conflict is widening. Israel has carried out strikes in Lebanon, while Washington is asking Iraq’s Kurds for support. FRANCE 24's Catherine Viette takes a look at the reactions and military deployments since Saturday.

items.title: What satellite images reveal about war in Iran
items.standfirst: Satellite images have revealed the scale of damage at Iranian military sites following US and Israeli strikes, but civilian infrastructure has also been hit, including schools and hospitals.
items.content: Satellite images have revealed the scale of damage at Iranian military sites following US and Israeli strikes, but civilian infrastructure has also been hit, including schools and hospitals.

items.title: War in the Middle East: Iran under heavy attack
items.standfirst: The United States and Israel have launched nationwide strikes on Iran, targeting military sites as well as civilian infrastructure. The war has killed at least 1,230 people in Iran, more than 70 in Lebanon and around a dozen in Israel, according to officials.
items.content: The United States and Israel have launched nationwide strikes on Iran, targeting military sites as well as civilian infrastructure. The war has killed at least 1,230 people in Iran, more than 70 in Lebanon and around a dozen in Israel, according to officials.

items.title: War in the Middle East: Where does France stand?
items.standfirst: Since US-Israeli strikes against Iran began last Saturday, France has taken what President Emmanuel Macron calls a “strictly defensive” posture. On Tuesday, Macron said the US-Israel operations in Iran were conducted “outside international law”, while announcing the deployment of the aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle and other military assets to the Mediterranean. So where does France stand? FRANCE 24’s Clovis Casali explains.
items.content: Since US-Israeli strikes against Iran began last Saturday, France has taken what President Emmanuel Macron calls a “strictly defensive” posture. On Tuesday, Macron said the US-Israel operations in Iran were conducted “outside international law”, while announcing the deployment of the aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle and other military assets to the Mediterranean. So where does France stand? FRANCE 24’s Clovis Casali explains.

ai_summary: War in Middle East widens with global reactions
US and Israel launch strikes on Iran
Iran launches retaliatory strikes on Israel
France takes a strictly defensive posture
Satellite images reveal damage to Iranian sites

clusters.title: Azerbaijan: Drone strike on an airport and near a school
clusters.neutral_headline: Azerbaijan Accuses Iran Of Drone Attack

items.title: Azerbaijan: Drone strike on an airport and near a school
items.standfirst: Two drones struck Azerbaijan on Thursday, injuring four people according to Baku, which accuses Iran and is threatening ‘retaliation’. Tehran, for its part, denies being behind the strikes and blames Israel instead.
items.content: Two drones struck Azerbaijan on Thursday, injuring four people according to Baku, which accuses Iran and is threatening ‘retaliation’. Tehran, for its part, denies being behind the strikes and blames Israel instead.

items.title: Azerbaijan accuses Iran of ‘terrorist’ drone attack on airport that injured four people
items.standfirst: Tehran denies responsibility but strike raises prospect of US-Israel war on Iran spreading beyond Middle EastMiddle East crisis – live updatesAzerbaijan has accused Iran of a “terrorist” drone attack that struck an airport and injured four civilians, raising concerns the conflict could spread beyond the Middle East.Azerbaijan’s defence ministry said Iran fired four drones at the country, one of which hit the terminal building at the only airport in Nakhchivan, an Azerbaijani exclave bordering Iran. A second drone fell close to a school in a nearby village, the ministry said. Continue reading...
items.content: Tehran denies responsibility but strike raises prospect of US-Israel war on Iran spreading beyond Middle EastMiddle East crisis – live updatesAzerbaijan has accused Iran of a “terrorist” drone attack that struck an airport and injured four civilians, raising concerns the conflict could spread beyond the Middle East.Azerbaijan’s defence ministry said Iran fired four drones at the country, one of which hit the terminal building at the only airport in Nakhchivan, an Azerbaijani exclave bordering Iran. A second drone fell close to a school in a nearby village, the ministry said. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Azerbaijan accuses Iran of drone attack

clusters.title: Oil price continues to rise amid Middle East crisis but stock markets rebound across Asia
clusters.neutral_headline: Oil Price Rises Amid Middle East Crisis

items.title: Oil price continues to rise amid Middle East crisis but stock markets rebound across Asia
items.standfirst: Reports of attack on US registered tanker in Gulf lifts crude by 3% to $84 a barrel as gas price also starts to climbBusiness live – latest updatesMiddle East crisis – live updatesStock markets have rebounded in Asia after days of heavy losses driven by the war in the Middle East, but oil and gas prices have continued to climb amid disruption to supplies.South Korea’s KOSPI, which posted its biggest ever fall on Tuesday of 12%, rose by almost 10% on Thursday, while Japan’s Nikkei climbed by 1.9%. MSCI’s Asia-Pacific index excluding Japan jumped by 2.7%. Continue reading...

MSCI’s Asia-Pacific index excluding Japan jumped by 2.7%. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: China sets lowest growth target since 1991 as economy struggles to keep momentum
clusters.neutral_headline: China Sets Lowest Growth Target Since 1991

items.title: China sets lowest growth target since 1991 as economy struggles to keep momentum
items.standfirst: China announced its lowest growth target in 35 years Thursday as the world’s second-biggest economy struggles with challenges at home and growing uncertainty around the world.
items.content: China announced its lowest growth target in 35 years Thursday as the world’s second-biggest economy struggles with challenges at home and growing uncertainty around the world.

items.title: China sets lowest economic growth target since 1991
items.standfirst: It is also the first time the target has been lowered since it was cut to "around 5%" in 2023.
items.content: It is also the first time the target has been lowered since it was cut to "around 5%" in 2023.

ai_summary: China sets lowest growth target since 1991
